name: Smartphone
description:
Resource cycle for a modern smartphone.

Graph nodes show rolled-up CO₂e (emissions only). Click a node for the step breakdown. The statement table compares Manufacture / Recycle / Reuse / Landfill pathways. Transformation is where ready modules become the finished device for the end user.
co2e_basis: Absolute process emissions only (no avoided-burden credits). Illustrative order-of-magnitude from electronics LCAs — not a certified EPD. Virgin path ≈ mining + fabs/assembly; recycle ≈ collect + shred/smelt recovery; reuse ≈ refurbish/repair. All routes emit CO₂e; reuse < recycle < virgin manufacture. Use-phase electricity is noted separately and is small vs manufacture for many grids.

nodes.label: Raw Material
nodes.summary: Mine and refine metals, minerals, and battery chemicals
nodes.details:
Copper, gold, lithium, cobalt, rare earths, and silica are extracted worldwide.

1. Mine ore and process concentrates.
2. Refine metals and battery precursors.
3. Ship refined inputs to component fabs.

Ore grade and region dominate upstream GHG and toxicity.
nodes.co2e_note: ~12 kg CO₂e/phone illustrative for mining + refining of metals and battery materials attributed to one device.

nodes.label: Manufacture
nodes.summary: Fabs, battery, display, and module build
nodes.details:
1. Semiconductor, display, and battery factories produce modules.
2. Contract manufacturers assemble boards and subassemblies.
3. Test and stage modules for final device Transformation.

Recovered metals/parts from Recycle rejoin component supply (folded into Recycle → Transformation as recovered feedstock ready to finish).
nodes.co2e_note: ~45 kg CO₂e/phone for chips, display, battery, and module manufacture — usually the largest share of lifetime GHG.

nodes.label: Recycle
nodes.summary: Collect, shred, smelt / separate → rejoin Transformation
nodes.details:
Take-back → shred and separate fractions → smelt precious/base metals → recovered feedstock back toward component/Transformation supply.

Figures are recovery-process emissions — not a credit for avoiding mining. Plastics and mixed fractions recover poorly; batteries need dedicated lines.
nodes.co2e_note: ~8 kg CO₂e/phone for collect/sort plus shredding and smelting recovery. Lower than virgin manufacture, still positive.

nodes.label: Reuse
nodes.summary: Repair, refurbish, resell — then back into Transformation
nodes.details:
Trade-in / certified refurbished: diagnostics → battery/screen repair → wipe & reflash → rejoin retail Transformation.

Each reuse path still emits (logistics + parts + labour energy), but far less than recycling or new manufacture. Spare parts and software support amplify this loop.
nodes.co2e_note: ~2.5 kg CO₂e/phone per refurbish cycle (logistics + typical battery/screen parts + test). Still emits, but less than recycle smelting.

nodes.label: Transformation
nodes.summary: Turn ready material into its final form for the end user
nodes.details:
Modules from Manufacture, recovered feedstock routes from Recycle, or refurbished units from Reuse are finally assembled/flashed/packed into the device the user receives.

Inbound modules and outbound retail freight are Deliver edges.
nodes.co2e_note: ~1.5 kg CO₂e/phone for final assembly, flash, QC, and pack.

nodes.label: Use
nodes.summary: Owner uses the phone; then choose landfill, recycle, or reuse
nodes.details:
Charging electricity is real but often smaller than manufacture over a few years on many grids. The important material choice is the next edge:

• Landfill — e-waste stockpile
• Recycle — shred/smelt / rejoin Transformation
• Reuse — repair/refurbish and rejoin Transformation
nodes.co2e_note: ~4 kg CO₂e/phone illustrative charging over a few years (grid-dependent). Manufacture still usually dominates.

nodes.label: Landfill
nodes.details: Drawer storage, residual waste, or informal dumps. Metals and plastics are lost to the product loop; leaching and fire risk rise in unmanaged piles.
nodes.co2e_note: ~0.4 kg CO₂e/phone collection + disposal ops. Climate cost of this route is that the next phone needs virgin manufacture.

edges.co2e_note: ~0.8 kg CO₂e/phone module logistics into final assembly.
~1.2 kg CO₂e/phone outbound air/sea + last-mile to user (illustrative).
